jokes.text: Am I interrupting something important? / Impossible. I work for the government.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if they're interrupting important work
jokes.punchline: Ron deadpans that nothing at government work could be important
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson character comedy - his libertarian anti-government stance delivered with typical deadpan precision

jokes.text: How are the girls-- / Yes, they are.
jokes.setup: Standard question setup about children's wellbeing
jokes.punchline: Ron answers a different question entirely - whether they ARE girls
jokes.explanation: Shows Ron's discomfort with children and social situations through literal interpretation

jokes.text: Her children are loud. / Kung fu! / Here comes trouble!
jokes.setup: Ron's confessional about the children being loud
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to children being loud
jokes.explanation: Simple comedic juxtaposition between Ron's statement and immediate proof

jokes.text: What the hell just happened?
jokes.setup: The chaos of children and social interaction
jokes.punchline: Character's bewildered reaction to the scene
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction comedy, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: But no trampoline room, correct? / Correct. Like all houses in the world, there's no trampoline room.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks about a ridiculous house feature
jokes.punchline: Realtor's matter-of-fact response about the universal lack of trampoline rooms
jokes.explanation: Captures Leslie's childlike enthusiasm and the realtor's professional patience with absurd requests

jokes.text: He would move into my place, but it's a scary nightmare hoarder nest. His words. And Ann's. And the official report filed by the Health Department.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains why Ben can't move into her place
jokes.punchline: Escalating sources of the same negative assessment
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure - from personal to friend to official government assessment

jokes.text: Thought it was gonna be more dramatic than that. / Hold on. Okay, say it again. / Martha, I wanna lease this house. / Whoo!
jokes.setup: Leslie realizes her big moment was anticlimactic
jokes.punchline: She stages a do-over with dramatic reaction
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's need for life to match her expectations of dramatic moments

jokes.text: It's just a piece of paper and he only made it for me after I specifically asked for it while crying loudly, but... It sure meant a lot to receive it.
jokes.setup: Chris proudly mentions his therapy award
jokes.punchline: Reveals the pathetic circumstances behind it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger - his relentless positivity about embarrassing situations

jokes.text: I hope no one minds if I live tweet this bitch.
jokes.setup: Announcement of a horror movie night
jokes.punchline: Donna's casual profanity and social media obsession
jokes.explanation: Typical Donna attitude - treats everything casually and makes it about social media

jokes.text: He holds my life in his hand like a fragile little bird.
jokes.setup: Chris talking about his therapist
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic and dependent description
jokes.explanation: Shows Chris's tendency toward dramatic metaphors and emotional dependency

jokes.text: But especially me! / Especially everyone. / This was a team effort, really. But I was the best. Thank you. / You were all fantastic. Most of all me, April Ludgate, the real hero. Thank you and you're welcome.
jokes.setup: Ben tries to give credit to everyone
jokes.punchline: April keeps redirecting credit to herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect April Ludgate - her complete lack of modesty and ability to make everything about herself

jokes.text: Gone, but never forgotten. / Who was that? I'm just kidding.
jokes.setup: Ben gives April a sentimental send-off
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts it by pretending he doesn't know her
jokes.explanation: Ben's dry humor and ability to deflate sentimental moments

jokes.text: Oh, for the last four years everything has been princesses. Every book, every movie, every backpack. All princesses all the time. / Oh, that sounds fun. / It's a nightmare.
jokes.setup: Diane explains her daughters' princess obsession, Andy responds positively
jokes.punchline: Diane's blunt contradiction
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent humor about children's obsessions

jokes.text: Tree, leaves, night, sky, hand. Andy's hand.
jokes.setup: Andy practicing observation skills for police academy
jokes.punchline: He observes his own hand
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy Dwyer - well-meaning but completely missing the point of the exercise

jokes.text: Some idiot kids were caught pooping on the soccer field?
jokes.setup: Diane explaining her vice principal emergency
jokes.punchline: The specific and gross nature of the school crisis
jokes.explanation: Decent absurd detail but relies somewhat on gross-out humor rather than character wit.

jokes.text: I need to go to the bathroom. / I-I have no idea what to do about that.
jokes.setup: Child announces bathroom need
jokes.punchline: Ron's complete helplessness with basic childcare
jokes.explanation: Ron Swanson's incompetence with children despite his general competence

jokes.text: In the fifth one, the canoe's actually the hero. It's a crazy twist.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Death Canoe movies
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous plot development of the canoe becoming the hero
jokes.explanation: Absurd horror movie logic played straight

jokes.text: Florida? They have sharks in Florida. And swamps. And swamp sharks. / Do you mean alligators? / Maybe.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Florida job opportunity
jokes.punchline: Leslie's irrational fear and confusion about Florida wildlife
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency to catastrophize and her geographical confusion

jokes.text: 7:34 pm. Man dressed as a nerd. Female dressed as crazy witch. Neither of us is in costume. Case closed. Candy, please.
jokes.setup: Andy doing police observation training while trick-or-treating
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong assessment and demanding candy
jokes.explanation: Andy's complete misunderstanding of social situations combined with police training

jokes.text: Okay, well, let's just even things out. There. Now neither of you has a tiara. Problem solved.
jokes.setup: Two girls fighting over broken tiara
jokes.punchline: Ron's solution is to break the other one too
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson problem-solving - logical but completely inappropriate for children

jokes.text: Now I know exactly how FDR felt after Pearl Harbor.
jokes.setup: Leslie feeling powerless about her situation
jokes.punchline: Comparing her relationship problems to WWII
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency to make dramatic historical comparisons for minor personal issues

jokes.text: Jerry, God. Gross. / Oh... Jerry? / Guys, I-- / Oh, God, I think he might be having a heart attack.
jokes.setup: Characters trying to scare someone in bathroom
jokes.punchline: They find Jerry having a medical emergency instead
jokes.explanation: Dark turn from prank to medical emergency

jokes.text: Oh, geez! Did a dinosaur just fart? / Jerry, get a grip. / Sorry, guys. / Ugh! Apology not accepted. / Stop talking, moron. / I wish I could stop smelling. / Dude. / Seriously, Jerry, did you eat farts for lunch?
jokes.setup: Jerry having a heart attack
jokes.punchline: Everyone more concerned with his flatulence than his health
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about prioritizing social embarrassment over medical emergency

jokes.text: I just want to hear the doctor say that Jerry had a fart attack. Is that too much to ask?
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining Jerry's heart attack
jokes.punchline: Tom's obsession with making it about flatulence
jokes.explanation: Tom's shallow priorities and need for everything to be entertaining

jokes.text: One time I sprained my wrist and our insurance claimed that having a wrist was a pre-existing condition.
jokes.setup: Explaining poor government health insurance
jokes.punchline: Absurd insurance logic about body parts being pre-existing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on insurance company logic - highly quotable and relatable

jokes.text: Honey, this is the hat I was wearing the first time I ever heard Vitalogy by Pearl Jam. / Oh, $900. / Yeah, that sounds about right.
jokes.setup: April suggests pricing a hat at 8 cents
jokes.punchline: Andy's sentimental attachment inflates the value absurdly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy-April dynamic - his earnest sentimentality meeting her support for absurd decisions

jokes.text: If the kids ever wanted to come to my place, I'd have to take a whole week off work just to undo the alarms and tripwires.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining relationship difficulties
jokes.punchline: His house is apparently booby-trapped
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson paranoia and self-reliance taken to extreme logical conclusion

jokes.text: Chris Traeger-- exercise phase. Andy Dwyer-- my grunge phase. Tom Haverford-- my needless shopping phase. Also my credit card debt phase.
jokes.setup: Ann cataloging her relationship phases
jokes.punchline: Tom gets two phases, both negative
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about Ann's adaptability and Tom's negative influence

jokes.text: As many of your know, Jerry Gergich, our friend, has suffered a devastating fart attack
jokes.setup: Leslie announcing Jerry's medical emergency
jokes.punchline: Using Tom's 'fart attack' terminology seriously
jokes.explanation: Effective callback to earlier joke, Leslie's earnestness makes it funnier

jokes.text: Well, that was my first high profile case. It was a classic Florida divorce. Guy cheats on his wife with Dan Marino's masseuse, one day she cuts his junk off, throws it in the Everglades. / Why not? / Damn alligator ate it.
jokes.setup: Lawyer explaining his career-making case
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd Florida divorce story
jokes.explanation: Dark Florida stereotype humor with good escalation and casual delivery

jokes.text: You can't sell clothes at a tag sale for more than they're worth. / Um, watch me.
jokes.setup: Customer questioning Tom's pricing
jokes.punchline: Tom's confident response to criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford entrepreneurial confidence despite questionable business practices

jokes.text: Do you wanna say anything to me? / Oh. Yes. You look nice today. / You broke my daughter's tiara. You ruined their Halloween. Don't you feel a little bit bad about that?
jokes.setup: Diane expecting an apology from Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron completely misses the point and gives a compliment instead
jokes.explanation: Ron's complete emotional obtuseness and inability to read social situations

jokes.text: I'm not gonna apologize. It wasn't even a real tiara, for God's sake. / Oh, well, relationship over. Too bad. / Oh, my God, you are so sad.
jokes.setup: Ron being stubborn about apologizing
jokes.punchline: Andy and April's blunt assessment of his emotional state
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics showing Ron's emotional immaturity

jokes.text: An evening with sexy nurse Ann Perkins. Look at her, folks. One evening with the most beautiful woman in the world.
jokes.setup: Leslie auctioning off Ann for charity
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top sales pitch for her best friend
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her friendship devotion and obliviousness to appropriateness.

jokes.text: Look at her, folks. One evening with the most beautiful woman in the world. Just dinner and dancing, nothing sexual. Unless she's into that, but no, she's not. Nothing fresh. Don't even try it. But you never know, right? / No! No, she's not into it. Don't try anything, but, you know, give it a shot.
jokes.setup: Leslie auctioning Ann for charity
jokes.punchline: Her contradictory and inappropriate auctioneering
jokes.explanation: Leslie's good intentions creating increasingly uncomfortable situation

jokes.text: Three out of four married couples have met each other at spontaneous auctions. / I don't think that's true. / I d--well, I don't know, I--
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to justify the auction
jokes.punchline: Her obviously made-up statistic and backing down
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency to make up facts to support her schemes

jokes.text: My cousin's got a kickass mud pit in his yard. She could watch me do belly flops then we maybe we could get some Thai food and a tank of nitrous and see what happens.
jokes.setup: Asking the high bidder about his date plans
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre and concerning plans
jokes.explanation: Escalating weirdness that justifies Leslie's protective instincts

jokes.text: I am going to bid a million dollars on myself. / Going once, going never, sold.
jokes.setup: Ann trying to escape awkward auction situation
jokes.punchline: Leslie's instant acceptance and modified auctioneer language
jokes.explanation: Good resolution to uncomfortable situation with clever wordplay

jokes.text: Rent-a-swag.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his teen clothing rental business
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous business name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford business venture - practical idea with ridiculous branding

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Jerry, but your future has been canceled. / Leslie, thank you. That is amazing. / No, it stinks. It's not even close to what you need. With all your bills, and the inevitable follow-up problems, and the lifetime of chronic misery that awaits you. I mean, you had this whole plan for your retirement and your beach house and now you're future is just a huge pile of crap.
jokes.setup: Leslie apologizing for insufficient fundraising
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly devastating assessment of Jerry's future
jokes.explanation: Leslie's brutal honesty contrasting with Jerry's gratitude creates dark comedy

jokes.text: I mean, you had this whole plan for your retirement and your beach house and now you're future is just a huge pile of crap.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to comfort Jerry about his medical bills
jokes.punchline: Her completely inappropriate honesty about his ruined future
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment showing her inability to be appropriately comforting while being brutally honest.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, your life is so depressing. / No thank you.
jokes.setup: Jerry inviting Leslie to watch TV and have Jell-o
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt assessment and rejection
jokes.explanation: Leslie's inability to hide her true feelings about Jerry's situation

jokes.text: I thought I could teach 'em to saw. / I now realize that seems dangerous.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to make amends with gifts for children
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate gift choice and delayed realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson - his idea of child-appropriate activities and slow social learning

jokes.text: I am deeply, ridiculously in love with you and above everything else, I just-- I wanna be with you forever.
jokes.setup: Ben making romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: The word 'ridiculously' in romantic context
jokes.explanation: Ben's nerdy vocabulary choice in emotional moment fits his character

jokes.text: Just--I need another second, please. I need to remember every little thing about how perfect my life is right now at this exact moment.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to propose
jokes.punchline: Leslie making him wait multiple times to savor the moment
jokes.explanation: Leslie's need to process and appreciate life moments fits her character perfectly

jokes.text: Trick or treat! / It's November 8th.
jokes.setup: Children showing up for trick-or-treating
jokes.punchline: Ben pointing out it's over a week late
jokes.explanation: Simple timing joke and Ben's literal mindset

jokes.text: All right, I got some, uh, calcium chews and, uh, a roll of cookie dough.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to provide Halloween candy
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate candy substitutes
jokes.explanation: Ben's practical but inappropriate response to unexpected situation
